perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to the Confidential Information to thos e of its officers, directors and employees who clearly need such access in order to participate on behalf of the Receiving Party in the analysis and negotiation of a business relationship or any contract or agreement, or the advisability thereof, with the Disclosing Party and who are bound by written confidentiality agreements that protect third party information.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall provide access to the Confidential Information to all of its employees, contractors, and consultants, regardless of their need to know. The Receiving Party is not required to ensure these parties are bound by written confidentiality agreements.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a significant contradiction. The original clause mandates restricted access to only those who 'clearly need such access' and are bound by confidentiality agreements. The modified clause mandates unrestricted access to all employees, contractors, and consultants, regardless of need and removes the requirement for confidentiality agreements. This creates a direct in-text contradiction regarding the level of access and the protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-Disclosure and Non -Use of Confidential Information . The Receiving Party shall not, without the prior written approval of the Disclosing Party in each instance or unless otherwise expressly permitted in this Agreement, use for its own benefit, publ ish or otherwise disclose to others, or permit the use by others for their benefit or to the detriment of the Disclosing Party, any of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party shall carefully restrict access to the Confidential Information to thos e of its officers, directors and employees who clearly need such access in order to participate on behalf of the Receiving Party in the analysis and negotiation of a business relationship or any contract or agreement, or the advisability thereof, with the Disclosing Party and who are bound by written confidentiality agreements that protect third party information. The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information if required by a judicial or governmental request, requirement or order; provided that the Receiving Party will take reasonable steps to give the Disclosing Party sufficient prior notice of such request, requirement or order for the Disclosing Party to contest, limit or protect such disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text. The original text and the contradicted text (which are the same in this case) both mandate restricted access to confidential information only to those with a 'clear need' and who are bound by confidentiality agreements. The changed text eliminates these restrictions, mandating access to all employees, contractors and consultants, regardless of need, and removes the requirement for confidentiality agreements. This creates a direct conflict with the original restriction, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable regarding access and confidentiality of information.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement is two (2) years from the Effective Date, provided that the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall expire five (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement is perpetual from the Effective Date, and the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information terminates immediately upon the termination of this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by changing the term and the survival of confidentiality obligations. Originally, the agreement lasted two years with confidentiality surviving for five years post-disclosure. The modified text makes the agreement perpetual but terminates confidentiality obligations upon termination, contradicting the survival clause and creating uncertainty about the duration of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Term. The term of this Agreement is two (2) years from the Effective Date, provided that the Receiving Party's duty to protect the Disclosing Party's Confidential Information shall survive expiration or termination of this Agreement and shall expire five (2) years from the date of disclosure.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the term of the agreement to 'perpetual' and terminates confidentiality upon agreement termination. The contradicted text states the agreement lasts two years, with confidentiality surviving for five years post-disclosure. The change directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding the duration of the agreement and the survival of confidentiality obligations, creating a strong in-text contradiction as defined by the 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement constitutes the sole understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may be amended or modified at any time by either party providing written notice to the other party.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction regarding the amendment process. The original clause requires a written agreement signed by both parties for any amendment. The modified clause allows unilateral amendment by either party with written notice. This contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement and introduces ambiguity about the amendment process, potentially leading to disputes over the validity of modifications. By removing the requirement of a signature from both parties, now either party can provide written notice, creating a direct in-text contradiction with the original terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Entire Agreement . This Agreement constitutes the sole understanding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and may not be amended or modified except in writing signed by each of the parties hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text allows for unilateral modification of the agreement with written notice, while the contradicted text requires a written agreement signed by both parties for any amendments. This creates a direct conflict regarding the amendment process, as the changed text removes the requirement for mutual agreement, leading to an in-text contradiction as either party can now modify the agreement with just written notice. The original text required the signature of each party, the changed text removes this requiring only a notice. Therefore there is an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby .
perturbation.changed_text: All Confidential Information created during the term of this agreement shall be jointly owned by both the Disclosing Party and the Receiving Party, and both parties shall have a non-exclusive license to use such Confidential Information.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by altering the ownership and rights to Confidential Information. The original clause asserts that all Confidential Information and intellectual property rights remain the property of the Disclosing Party, with no license granted. The modified clause states that Confidential Information created during the agreement's term is jointly owned, with both parties having a non-exclusive license. This directly contradicts the original ownership clause and creates uncertainty about who owns and has the right to use the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Ownership of Confidential Information . All Co nfidential Information and all intellectual property rights therein shall remain the property of the Disclosing Party and no license or other right to Confidential Information is granted or implied hereby .
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the contradicted text regarding the ownership and licensing of Confidential Information. The original and contradicted text states that the Disclosing Party retains all ownership and no license is granted. The changed text asserts joint ownership and a non-exclusive license, creating a clear and irreconcilable conflict regarding the rights to Confidential Information. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.
